framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Build open-source bioinformatics tools microbiologists run their analyses on
paths.what_it_is: Design, build, and maintain open-source software that microbiologists use daily to process, denoise, and analyze sequencing data, released out of a research lab rather than a company.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can turn your science into software the field actually runs its work on — impact that reaches far beyond your own study or paper.

paths.path: Develop and publish a standardized lab protocol the field adopts wholesale
paths.what_it_is: Design, validate, and publish a wet-lab protocol — sample collection, extraction, or sequencing — precise and reproducible enough that other labs adopt it directly instead of writing their own.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves your method is reproducible enough for strangers to run unsupervised and get the same result — the strongest test of scientific rigor there is.

paths.path: Communicate microbiology to a public audience via podcast or blog
paths.what_it_is: Build an ongoing public-facing show, blog, or newsletter that explains microbiology or infectious disease to a lay audience, alongside your formal training.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you understand the science well enough to make it make sense to people outside the field — and puts your name in front of audiences your resume alone never reaches.
